name: Lock a quote and continue into contract terms
description: Happy path through phase 1 → phase 2. Quote is created, locked, then the assistant offers a 'Set up the contract?' continue CTA which transitions to terms phase.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: O'Brien deck build — quote already drafted in this conversation.

preconditions: Active thread with a draft quote already created in a prior turn

steps.expect: lock_quote action fires; chat shows the quote moving to sent/locked state; an action_card displays the locked quote
Inline CTA card reads 'Set up the contract?' with primary 'Yes, let's go' and secondary 'Not yet'
Phase transitions to 'terms'; assistant prompts for deposit, schedule, exclusions one at a time
A draft contract for the O'Brien job is now listed

evaluationCriteria.check: Continue CTA is visually anchored — clearly the next action, not buried in text
Phase transition feels intentional; the chat header or banner reflects 'Now setting up contract terms'
If the user dismisses the CTA, returning to the thread still shows the quote was locked — phase falls back to 'quote' cleanly
Continue CTA is reachable via Tab; both buttons announce their role
